step_a.description: Forward verification: PART2 V2 claims -> SOT

details.note: Exact match with SOT D2.0-07 cost table. AGENT_TEAMS_SPEC 11.2.1 also confirms 93,000/month.
Consistent across all SOT sources.

PART2 uses 'L3_AUTONOMOUS', SOT D2.0-07 defines L3 as 'FULL_AUTO'. Naming convention difference. PART2 autonomy naming does not exactly match D2.0-07 enum names (L0=FULL_MANUAL, L1=SUPERVISED, L2=SEMI_AUTO, L3=FULL_AUTO).
PART2 adds Neo4j for KG, consistent with V2 Phase 1 migration plan. D2.0-06 mentions Postgres+Qdrant for storage, Neo4j is added as implementation choice.
2-tier is consistent with cost strategy. AGENT_TEAMS_SPEC 9.2 confirms Lead=Opus, Sub=Sonnet/Haiku.

details.sot_ref: SDAR_SPEC section 1.4: V2=COND(AR-L2 base, AR-L3 conditional). Section 10.2 confirms AR-L3 for V2.
details.note: SDAR_SPEC V2 scope includes AR-L3 MEDIUM risk repairs.

details.claim: V2 adds +10 COND modules: I-7, I-12, I-22, I-23, I-25, A-4, E-13~E-16
details.note: Module IDs verified against SOT. I-7(ProjectSessionMgr), I-12(WorkflowBuilder), I-22(TaskMgr), I-23(DocStructuring), I-25(SDAR), A-4(DebateMode), E-13~E-16(Calendar/Email/Cloud/Storage).

details.claim: V2 infra = VPS + Docker Compose (Hetzner recommended)
details.sot_ref: No direct SOT for Hetzner recommendation
details.note: Hetzner recommendation is PART2-specific implementation guidance. No SOT document specifies VPS vendor. This is appropriate implementation-level detail.

details.part2_ref: Not explicitly stated in V2 section header; inferred from Agent Teams LOCK-AT tables
details.note: PART2 section 4 (V2) does not explicitly state V2 Lead Agent model upgrade to Opus. This was identified in Phase 2 as F-A4-01 and remains unresolved.

details.claim: V2 migration: SQLite->PostgreSQL, JSONL->PostgreSQL, Chroma->Qdrant, JSON->Neo4j
details.note: Migration paths align with SOT storage roadmap.
10-step process matches PHASE_B7 specification.

details.claim: V2 SDAR AR-L3 adds 5 MEDIUM risk repair actions (RA_006-RA_010)
details.note: 5 MEDIUM actions match exactly: patch_prompt_template, update_config_parameter, rotate_api_key, rollback_to_snapshot, compress_logs.

details.claim: V2 memory activation: L1 default ON, L2 restricted, L3 restricted
details.part2_ref: Inferred from V2 Phase 2 module descriptions
details.sot_ref: D2.0-06 section 2.3: V2 L1=active, L2=restricted(approval required), L3=restricted
details.note: Consistent with D2.0-06 P1-SCOPE activation table.

details.claim: V2 Vector DB adapter: QdrantAdapter (primary), PgvectorAdapter (fallback)
details.note: Adapter interface and implementation choices match SOT.

details.sot_ref: AGENT_TEAMS_SPEC section 11.2.1: V2 per-task budget = 2,000 KRW
details.note: PART2 does not mention per-task budget. Only daily/monthly limits are stated.

details.claim: V2 Agent Teams: max 10 agents, max 10 parallel
details.note: LOCK-AT-014 in PART2 states V2 parallel limit=10, consistent with SOT.

details.part2_ref: PART2 Agent Teams section 2.3.0: V2+ = RedisMessageBus
details.note: Content matches but PART2 places this in the general Agent Teams section (section 6.7), not explicitly in V2 section (section 4). Minor organizational issue.

step_b.description: Reverse verification: SOT V2 critical facts -> PART2 presence

details.note: V2 section in PART2 does not explicitly state Lead=Opus. Phase 2 finding F-A4-01 still unresolved.
Per-task budget not mentioned in PART2. Phase 2 finding F-A4-02 still unresolved.

details.sot_fact: V2 collaboration patterns: Sequential, Parallel, Debate, Supervisor, Handoff (AGENT_TEAMS_SPEC 9.2)
details.note: PART2 V2 Phase 2 references Debate Mode (A-4) and Agent Teams patterns.

details.sot_fact: V2 SDAR: Anomaly Detection Channel C added (SDAR_SPEC 10.2)
details.note: PART2 V2 SDAR section mentions AR-L3 actions but does not explicitly list Channel C Anomaly Detection as V2 addition.

details.sot_fact: V2 storage: L2 activation requires approval (S03-MOD-001, D2.0-06 2.3)
details.note: Implied through memory/storage architecture sections.
Agent lifecycle states not described in PART2 V2 section. Phase 2 finding F-A4-03 persistent.
Shown in version comparison table.
Referenced via R10 rule and cost gate mechanisms.

details.sot_fact: V2 Agent RBAC mapping table (AGENT_TEAMS_SPEC 6.5.1 LOCK)
details.note: Phase 2 finding from v8 Agent 4 MISSING #2. Agent-specific RBAC permissions not detailed in PART2.

details.sot_fact: V2 COND module activation conditions: QoD >= 0.85, RAG accuracy >= 60% (PART2 own requirement)
details.note: Stated in V2-Phase 2 user guide section L3207.

details.sot_fact: V2 embedding model: BGE-M3 Matryoshka 256dim (D2.0-06 DEC-005)
details.note: Referenced in Chroma->Qdrant migration section L2795.
A-4 Debate Mode listed as V2 COND module in PART2 L3264.
10-Step Orchestration is well-defined. Alembic-based migration with batch processing (1000 records) is standard practice. Rollback functions included.

details.check: V2 cost ceiling 93,000 KRW ($70) covers infra + LLM + tools
details.note: Hetzner CX31 ~$8/month + LLM API costs within budget. Docker Compose eliminates managed service fees. Cost monitoring dashboard included.

details.check: V2 116 COND modules (10 original + 106 v10) implementable in 6 weeks (Phase 2 Week 4-9)
details.note: 116 modules in 6 weeks = ~19 modules/week. Even with batch implementation via Mixin patterns and 7-category grouping, this is aggressive. The 106 v10 modules rely on BaseModule + Mixin pattern, but each still needs testing. Risk of quality degradation under time pressure.

details.check: V2 Chroma->Qdrant migration with 30-day Dual-Collection feasible
details.note: 4-Phase approach (create collection, re-embed, dual-run, cutover) is sound. BGE-M3 re-embedding with FlagEmbedding is standard. HNSW parameters (ef_construct=128, m=16) are reasonable.

details.check: V2 SDAR AR-L3 depends on S-4 Error Pattern Miner (SDAR_SPEC 10.2)
details.note: SDAR_SPEC 10.2 lists S-4 integration as V2 requirement, but S-4 is classified as V3 EXP module (S-2~S-8 are V3 per PART2 V3-Phase 2 group 4). Dependency conflict: V2 SDAR AR-L3 needs S-4, but S-4 is V3. PART2 should clarify whether a V2-minimal S-4 stub is sufficient.

details.check: V2 Neo4j Community deployment within Docker Compose
details.note: Neo4j Community 5.x has official Docker image. Bolt protocol (7687) + HTTP (7474) ports well-defined. Integration with existing VAMOS modules is feasible.

details.check: V2 Redis MessageBus for Agent Teams communication
details.note: Redis 7+ in Docker Compose. Pub/Sub for multi-agent messaging is standard. HMAC signature validation adds minimal overhead.

findings.description: V2 Lead Agent model upgrade to Opus not explicitly stated in PART2 section 4
findings.evidence: AGENT_TEAMS_SPEC 9.2 and 11.2.1 clearly define V2 Lead=Opus. PART2 V2 section omits this. Previously identified as F-A4-01 in Phase 2.

findings.description: V2 per-task budget (2,000 KRW) from AGENT_TEAMS_SPEC 11.2.1 not reflected in PART2
findings.evidence: SOT defines V1=500/task, V2=2000/task, V3=5000/task. PART2 only mentions daily/monthly limits.

findings.description: Agent lifecycle states (created->initialized->active->suspended->terminated->archived) from AGENT_TEAMS_SPEC 2.4.1 LOCK not described in PART2 V2 section
findings.evidence: SOT provides detailed lifecycle with LogEvent mappings. PART2 section 4 omits this entirely.

findings.description: Agent RBAC mapping table from AGENT_TEAMS_SPEC 6.5.1 LOCK not included in PART2
findings.evidence: SOT defines can_delegate/can_approve/can_access_p2/tool_access per agent type. PART2 lacks per-agent permission matrix.

findings.description: V2 autonomy level naming: PART2 'L3_AUTONOMOUS' vs D2.0-07 'FULL_AUTO'
findings.evidence: PART2 L108 uses L3_AUTONOMOUS, SOT D2.0-07 3.2 defines L3 as FULL_AUTO.

findings.description: V2 116 COND modules in 6-week Phase 2 is aggressive schedule
findings.evidence: 116 modules / 6 weeks = ~19/week. Even with Mixin pattern batching, this risks quality issues. v10 106 modules categorized into 7 groups (CAT-A through CAT-G), but individual testing overhead remains.

findings.description: V2 SDAR AR-L3 requires S-4 Error Pattern Miner, but S-4 is classified as V3 EXP module
findings.evidence: SDAR_SPEC 10.2 lists S-4 as V2 requirement. PART2 V3-Phase 2 group 4 places S-2~S-8 in V3. Dependency not resolved.

findings.description: V2 SDAR Anomaly Detection Channel C not explicitly listed as V2 addition in PART2
findings.evidence: SDAR_SPEC 10.2 V2 scope includes 'Layer 1: Anomaly Detection (Channel C) added'. PART2 I-25 V2 description focuses on AR-L3 actions only.

resolved: B-1 to B-4 (Kill Switch, LOCK 9 items, Category E, P2 limits) - all confirmed MATCH in v8, remain valid
B-12 (AR-L0~L4 14 repair actions) - confirmed MATCH, action counts accurate
new: F-A3-06 (V2 116 modules in 6 weeks feasibility concern)
F-A3-07 (S-4 dependency conflict between V2 SDAR and V3 S-Series)
persistent: F-A3-01 (V2 Lead=Opus missing) - persistent from Phase 2 F-A4-01
F-A3-03 (Agent lifecycle missing) - persistent from Phase 2 F-A4-03
F-A3-04 (Agent RBAC missing) - persistent from v8 Agent 4 MISSING #2
F-A3-05 (Autonomy level naming) - new finding, related to general naming consistency
